evals.prompt: Enable group scanning in my MatrixScan Count app. File: IntegratedBarcodeCountViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.groupScanningEnabled = true on the BarcodeCountSettings before the BarcodeCount mode is constructed, which adds the Next Group / Redo controls to the built-in UI. The existing symbologies and the rest of the integration are unchanged.

assertions.text: settings.groupScanningEnabled = true is set on the BarcodeCountSettings
It is set before BarcodeCount(context:settings:) is constructed
It uses the real property groupScanningEnabled — not a guessed API or a view property
The existing enabled symbologies remain enabled; the camera, mode, view, listener, uiDelegate and lifecycle are unchanged

evals.prompt: Add group scanning to my MatrixScan Count app, and label the Next Group control "Next pallet" and the Redo control "Clear group". File: IntegratedBarcodeCountViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill enables group scanning (settings.groupScanningEnabled = true on the BarcodeCountSettings before the mode is constructed) and customizes the control labels via barcodeCountView.setNextGroupButtonText("Next pallet") and barcodeCountView.setRedoButtonText("Clear group"). The rest of the integration is unchanged.

assertions.text: Group scanning is enabled via settings.groupScanningEnabled = true so the controls are visible
It uses the real text setters (setNextGroupButtonText / setRedoButtonText) — not guessed property names; the rest of the integration is unchanged

evals.prompt: With group scanning on in MatrixScan Count, how do I read the scanned barcodes for each group separately?
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that group scanning is a UI/workflow aid only — there is NO grouped-result callback. Scanned barcodes still arrive as a flat list through the normal BarcodeCountListener.barcodeCount(_:didScanIn:frameData:) (session.recognizedBarcodes), so per-group bookkeeping must be done in the app's own logic. It does not invent a group/result API.

assertions.text: It states there is no grouped-result callback / API for group scanning
It says results still come back as a flat list via the existing BarcodeCountListener (barcodeCount(_:didScanIn:frameData:) / session.recognizedBarcodes)
It suggests tracking per-group state in the app's own logic if needed
It does NOT invent a grouped-results API (no session.groups / didFinishGroup / per-group callback)
